title: equisetum variegatum f. caespitosum
intro: Equisetum variegatum f. caespitosum, also known as rough horsetail, is a subspecies of the Equisetum variegatum plant, which belongs to the class Equisetopsida and is one of the most ancient vascular plants on earth. It has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic effects, which are attributed to its high silica content and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of rough horsetail include the treatment of urinary tract infections, kidney stones, and gout, as well as its ability to improve bone density and reduce inflammation. In herbal preparations, rough horsetail is often used as a powder or tincture, and can be added to teas, salves, or creams for topical application.
